audit_status.source_base: Greek Colossians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for masters, prayer, mystery, wise speech, Tychicus/Onesimus, greetings, Archippus, final grace.

verses.restored: Masters, provide what is righteous/just and equal/fair to enslaved ones, knowing that you also have a Master in heaven.
Persevere in prayer, keeping awake in it with thanksgiving,
praying at the same time also concerning us, that the Divine may open to us a door of the word, to speak the mystery of Messiah, because of which I have also been bound,
so that I may make it manifest, as it is necessary for me to speak.
Walk in wisdom toward those outside, redeeming the season.
Let your word always be in grace, seasoned with salt, to know how it is necessary for you to answer each one.
Tychicus, the beloved sibling and trustworthy servant and fellow enslaved one in the Lord, will make known to you all the things concerning me.
I sent him to you for this very thing, so that you may know the things concerning us and that he may comfort/encourage your hearts,
with Onesimus, the trustworthy and beloved sibling, who is from you. They will make known to you all the things here.
Aristarchus my fellow prisoner greets you, and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas, concerning whom you received commands; if he comes to you, receive him,
and Jesus called Justus. These are from the circumcision, only these coworkers into the reign of the Divine, who became comfort/encouragement to me.
Epaphras, who is from you, servant of Messiah Jesus, greets you, always struggling on your behalf in prayers, so that you may stand mature/complete and fully assured in all will of the Divine.
For I bear witness to him that he has much labor for you and those in Laodicea and those in Hierapolis.
Luke, the beloved physician, greets you, and Demas.
Greet the siblings in Laodicea, and Nympha and the assembly in her house.
And when the letter has been read among you, make it also be read in the assembly of Laodiceans, and that you also read the one from Laodicea.
And say to Archippus: See the service/ministry that you received in the Lord, so that you may fulfill it.
The greeting by my hand, Paul's. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.

translation_notes: Colossians 4:1 continues the household code but sharply relativizes master power under the heavenly Master and calls for justice/equality.
Onesimus is named trustworthy and beloved sibling; this should be handled with dignity and in connection to Philemon.
Nympha's house-assembly is preserved as a likely woman-hosted assembly.
